title: Cleft sentences
explanation:
Cleft sentences

Cleft sentences emphasise one part of a sentence.

  • It is/was ... that/who: It was Maria who solved the problem.
  • What ... is/was: What I need is a short break.
  • All ... is/was: All I want is an honest answer.

questions.text: It [*was|is|were] the manager who made the final decision.
questions.feedback: Past emphasis with It was ... who.

questions.text: [*What|That|Which] surprised me most was her confidence.
It [*is|was|are] honesty that matters most in this job.
[*All|Every|Each] I asked for was five more minutes.
It [*was|is|were] in 2019 that the company expanded abroad.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: It was the poor lighting ____ made photography difficult. | *that | who | where
What we need now ____ a clear plan. | *is | are | was
It was Julia ____ recommended the software. | *who | which | where
All he wanted ____ to be left alone. | *was | were | is
It [*was|is|were] because of the storm that the flight was cancelled.
questions.feedback: Reason emphasised with It was because ...

questions.text: [*What|That|Which] I cannot understand is their silence.
It [*is|was|are] your attitude that worries me.
[*All|Every|Both] she did was smile and walk away.
It [*was|is|were] the first edition that collectors value most.
